title: Former Los Angeles Lakers star named most overrated player in NBA
description: A former Los Angeles Lakers star has been named the NBA's most overrated player.  Davis, 33, was recently given the distinction of being the league's most overrated player by Zach Buckley of Bleacher Report, who pointed to Davis' inconsistent availability and declining production as reasons why he topped the list.
fullText:
Former Los Angeles Lakers star named most overrated player in NBA originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

A former Los Angeles Lakers star has been named the NBA's most overrated player.

That player is Washington Wizards forward Anthony Davis, who was one of the best players in the league at one point, but has been hampered by injury issues as of late.

Davis, 33, was recently given the distinction of being the league's most overrated player by Zach Buckley of Bleacher Report, who pointed to Davis' inconsistent availability and declining production as reasons why he topped the list.

"If guaranteed a clean bill of health, would Anthony Davis be the most impactful player on this list? Maybe. But that's a moot point when he can be guaranteed nothing of the sort," Buckley wrote.

". . . Things that used to be automatic with AD are, at best, major question marks. He'll have flashes of his old dominant days and they might get covered similarly since he's such a notable name, but when his employer is merely hoping to get 50 games from him at this point (a number he missed in three of the past six seasons), things have clearly changed."

Davis will have an opportunity to prove Buckley, and other doubters, wrong this upcoming season, which will be his first full season as a member of the Washington Wizards.

title: Shaquille O'Neal: LeBron James Ending Career With Cavaliers Would Be 'Historical'
description: Shaquille O'Neal thinks it would be cool to see LeBron James finish his NBA career with the Cleveland Cavaliers.
fullText:
NBA legend Shaquille O'Neal believes LeBron James ending his career with the Cleveland Cavaliers would be "historical."

LeBron began his NBA career with the Cavaliers back in 2003. He played for the franchise from 2003-10 before going to the Miami Heat from 2010-14. The Akron native then returned to Cleveland from 2014-18 and helped delivered the team's first title in franchise history in 2016.

A future Hall of Famer, LeBron is currently a free agent for the first time since 2018. He's expected to sign with the Cavaliers, Golden State Warriors, Philadelphia 76ers or Minnesota Timberwolves.

Many people believe LeBron will sign with the Cavaliers. Cleveland assistant general manager Brandon Weems was with LeBron in Ohio a few weekends ago.

Weems is one of LeBron's closest friends.

"Let me talk to you about Brandon Weems," LeBron's agent, Rich Paul of Klutch Sports Group, said on his podcast. "Brandon Weems is basically LeBron's brother. Now, he worked his own way [up], nobody gave him anything. He's earned to be in the front office of the Cavs. But that is a big feather in the cap [for Cleveland], because of Brandon Weems."

It would certainly be cool to see LeBron end his career in Cleveland. A starting lineup of James Harden, Donovan Mitchell, LeBron, Evan Mobley and Jarrett Allen could compete for the championship next season.

The Cavaliers made the conference finals last season, losing to the New York Knicks in four quick games. Adding LeBron could make Cleveland title favorites next season.

If Harden signs for $30 million and if Cleveland can deal Max Strus and take back no salary in return, the Cavaliers would be able to give LeBron the mid-level exception.

For the Los Angeles Lakers last season, LeBron averaged 20.9 points, 6.1 rebounds and 7.2 assists per game. He's still an elite offensive player despite being the oldest active player in the NBA.

It will be interesting to see where LeBron is playing next season. Paul recently said on his podcast that LeBron will not be "rushed" into making a decision.

"We're not going to be rushed," Paul said on the latest podcast episode of "Game Over" with Max Kellerman and Rich Paul, released Monday evening. "We're not going to be rushed. It's his choice to make and when he makes the choice, he'll make it. ... I saw Adam in New York and we discussed it. And like I said, it's still his choice. So if he doesn't really know, you can't rush it. You can't rush it."

title: NBA Free Agency: More Jalen Duren Intel From Summer League
description: The Detroit Pistons are still working on a new deal for All-Star center Jalen Duren, but it is clear that a sign-and-trade will not be in store this summer
fullText:
Over the last several weeks, the basketball world has converged in Las Vegas for the Summer League. The Detroit Pistons had both of their draft picks in the lineup for each game, and essentially the whole team was there to support the Summer League squad even though they were only able to produce one victory (and four losses) in the series of exhibition games.

The word essentially was used in describing the Pistons’ team-wide support because 22-year old All-Star center Jalen Duren was notably absent from that contingent, but that is no surprise considering he’s still embroiled in contract negotiations with the club.

So far, there has not been an overwhelming amount of progress on either side. Duren would like a significant raise that is at least in the ballpark of a max contract, but the Pistons would feel more comfortable with a more team-friendly deal that allows for financial flexibility moving forward, which will be key since Ausar Thompson will be up next for a contract extension in due time.

In order to help understand that fluid situation, Spotrac’s Keith Smith spoke with multiple people within the Pistons organization, and he learned just where Detroit stands at the moment amid the important contract discussions being held with Duren and his representation.

“We very clearly want Jalen back with us for next year and beyond,” Smith’s front office source said. “If we didn’t, we could have easily done several different sign-and-trade deals. But the numbers have to be right. You saw what’s happened with Boston and OKC and New York. We don’t want to have to shed talent around Cade [Cunningham] and Jalen a year or two from now.”

Based on that statement, Pistons President of Basketball Operations Trajan Langdon is operating with the understanding that he will need to work out a contract extension with Thompson sooner rather than later, and he’d prefer not to handcuff Detroit before those discussions even begin. Right now, the Pistons have more than enough money to allocate for Duren’s upcoming deal, but they want to remain in a position to offer Thompson what he’s worth at the same time. But in any event, the Pistons have zero disillusions over the value that Duren adds to the team, so it is overwhelmingly likely that the two sides will come to an agreement in the near future.

“JD is a monster,” Smith was told by a Detroit assistant coach. “I don’t think any big man in the league has improved as much as JD has in his first four years in the league. Go look at his rookie year. He didn’t know what to do on defense, so he didn’t do much of anything. Last two years, he’s defending at a high level. He reads the floor well on offense. He’s super athletic. I hope everyone can be reasonable and we have him forever in Detroit.”

As a big man who averaged 19.5 points and 10.5 rebounds last season en route to an All-NBA Third Team selection, Duren is still adding to his skill set. That much was clear since Duren was a markedly improved shooter from the free throw line during the 2025-26 campaign, and he ended up finishing in second place for the Most Improved Player award as a result of his hard work.

Now, it’s the Pistons’ job to make sure that Duren continues working hard in the Motor City as opposed to any other location across the league.

title: LeBron’s Free Agency Continues to Stall NBA Offseason
description: James’s ongoing free agency has lasted longer than his previous three combined.
fullText:
LeBron James continues to hold up the rest of the NBA while he decides on his next team.

It has been 23 days since James parted ways with the Lakers after eight seasons, leading to nearly a month full of speculation as to where the 41-year-old will play next.

NBA commissioner Adam Silver recently said at a CNBC event last week that the league can’t formally set its schedule without knowing where one of its biggest stars will be playing.

“We have to finish up the schedule, and where LeBron plays affects the schedule,” Silver said. “It will influence how we set the schedule—opening week, Christmas. So I need him to make a decision.”

But James is also holding up the rest of free agency around the league as several key players remain unsigned, with the teams interested in them remaining in a holding pattern until the NBA’s all-time leading scorer makes up his mind.

A common denominator among some of the main unsigned free agents: They are also represented by Jame’s agent in Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul.

In most years, the NBA goes into hibernation in late July. The league’s personnel and media descend on Las Vegas for Summer League when most of free agency is wrapped up and then head out on vacation until the regular season comes within view in September.

But James is extending the offseason and is holding up deals for multiple teams and potential teammates as he ponders his destination.

“We’re definitely working later than usual,” one front office executive told Front Office Sports.

James’s free agency has extended longer than any of his previous ones combined. In 2010, he went eight days before announcing he would sign with the Heat during ‘The Decision.’ The time between his departure from Miami and return to Cleveland four years later lasted 10 days. And it took just 18 hours for James to announce he was heading west to Los Angeles in 2018 .

All of James’s free agencies began with an ending in mind. He ended his first one on ESPN, second with a Sports Illustrated op-ed, and third through the Klutch Sports X account. But the length of his current free agency leads one to wonder whether it has the gameplan his previous ones had.

First in line for new deals after James decides are James Harden (Cavaliers) and Draymond Green (Warriors). Harden declined his $42.3 million player option for next season on June 29, the day before James parted ways with the Lakers, to sign a long-term deal in Cleveland. Harden, who turns 37 next month, wants an NBA championship and is reportedly willing to sign for less money to have James alongside him.

The Cavaliers could potentially offer James the full mid-level exception of $15 million if Harden takes a pay cut and if the team trades Jarrett Allen and Max Strus. But if James is fine taking the veteran minimum of roughly $4 million, it wouldn’t be necessary.

Then there’s the case of Green, who declined his $27 million player option with the Warriors in an attempt to help lure James to Golden State. The Warriors can offer James the full mid-level exception without performing any trades, but it would require Green to sign for less. Green is another Klutch client James is keeping in limbo.

Peyton Watson, another Klutch client, and Jalen Duren, round out some of the other major un-signed free agents all awaiting a decision from James.

The post LeBron’s Free Agency Continues to Stall NBA Offseason appeared first on Front Office Sports.

title: Toppin returns to Dayton for sixth annual basketball camp
description: Jul. 23—Obi Toppin has added drivers, irons and a putter to a bag of tricks that includes his signature between-the-legs EastBay dunk. The event benefited the Toppin Family Foundation, which he founded earlier this month. Toppin's mom, Roni ...
fullText:
Jul. 23—Obi Toppin has added drivers, irons and a putter to a bag of tricks that includes his signature between-the-legs EastBay dunk.

The former Dayton Flyers forward, now preparing for his seventh season in the NBA, held the Obi Open on Wednesday, July 22, 2026, at NCR Country Club. The event benefited the Toppin Family Foundation, which he founded earlier this month. Toppin's mom, Roni Toppin, is the president of the foundation.

The foundation's mission, according to its website, is to "create opportunity where it is needed most — supporting underprivileged children and families while investing in student-athletes who face financial hardship. We believe access to support, education, and athletics can change lives and strengthen our community for generations to come."

Toppin said they created the foundation to help the next generation. He had some struggles as a kid and wants to give kids a "better path to their dreams."

The golf outing was one of the foundation's first fundraising events. Toppin still shares many more basketball photos than golf photos on his Instagram profile, but the game is growing on him.

"My golf game is getting better every day," Toppin said. "It's all right. I can hold my own. I'm not the best golfer, but I'm getting better every single day."

Toppin, the consensus national player of the year in his final season at Dayton in 2020, wasn't the only former Dayton All-American on the course. DaRon Holmes II, who arrived at Dayton one year after Toppin departed, also played in the event.

"He told me it was his first time on a golf course," Toppin said. "I'm like, 'No way.' So I've got to get out there with him more and teach him golf."

Holmes signed autographs and posed for photos with members of the Flyer Faithful a day earlier at Lee's Famous Recipe Chicken in Kettering.

Obi Toppin: A tribute to a new Dayton Flyers hall of famer

Toppin returned to Dayton not only for the golf outing but for his sixth annual basketball camp. The Obi Toppin Basketball ProCamp, sponsored by CareSource, welcomed approximately 150 kids in grades one through eight to the University of Dayton RecPlex on Thursday. The camp continues Friday.

Toppin started the camp at the Bales Center in Beavercreek in 2021. In 2022, it moved to UD, where it has been held ever since.

"It's amazing," Toppin said. "I see a lot of familiar faces. I feel like it gets better and better every year, and it looks like there are more kids than previous years."

Toppin planned to head straight from the camp Thursday to speak to Dayton's current players at the Cronin Center. He met some of the players earlier in the week at a dinner.

Toppin could speak to the players about any number of experiences he's had in his career, including overcoming injuries. A foot injury marred his sixth NBA season and third with the Indiana Pacers. He played in the first three games of the season but didn't return to games until Feb. 26. He then appeared in 21 of the last 23 games.

One season after losing to the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 7 of the NBA Finals, the Pacers finished with the second-worst record in the NBA and the worst record in franchise history (19-63). The team's star player, Tyrese Haliburton, missed the entire season after tearing his Achilles tendon in Game 7 last summer.

"There were a lot of injuries last year, but we have a healthier team this year," Toppin said. "We've got a great group of guys coming back, and I feel like our group has a really good chance."

The struggles of the Pacers coincided with the rise of Toppin's former team, the New York Knicks, who won the NBA championship one year after being eliminated by the Pacers in the Eastern Conference finals.

"I'm obviously close to the guys on that team, so I'm super happy for them," Toppin said. "They worked really hard to get to where they had to get to."

Now Toppin's goal is for the Pacers to follow the Knicks as champions.

"We've got to go get us one," Toppin said.

title: What team is LeBron James going to? LeBron James next team odds
description: The Philadelphia 76ers are among a handful of teams making a pitch to sign free the NBA legend. See Lebron James next team odds.
fullText:
Do the Philadelphia 76ers really have a shot?

Following the announcement he wouldn't re-sign with the Los Angeles Lakers, NBA legend LeBron James has made it clear to teams that he would consider signing with a franchise that best fit his needs.

What those are aren't exactly clear, but the Sixers are reportedly one of the teams in the mix for James' services for the upcoming 2026-2027 NBA season — and possibly beyond.

See where the Sixers currently stand among James' suitors.

As of July 23, the Sixers ranked among the top possible teams to sign the all-time NBA leading scorer, according to prediction market Kalshi.

Here's where they rank as of 9 a.m. ET July 23:

Cleveland Cavaliers 27% (down 1%)

Golden State Warriors 17% (up 3%)

Minnesota Timberwolves 2% (down 1%)

Here's the breakdown of James' current odds of signing, according to market prediction site Polymarket:

Cleveland Cavaliers 25% (down 162%)

Golden State Warriors 17% (down 9%)

The Miami Heat accidentally posted a scheduled YouTube livestream titled “LeBronJames Introductory Press Conference” for July 27, a team spokesperson told ESPN's Shams Charania.

The link was mistakenly published while preparing for a possible James signing. It has since been deleted.

While most believe James will return to his hometown Cleveland to close out his career, there's a chance a he turns down a third reunion with the Cavs and signs elsewhere.

Recently, James' agent Rich Paul of Klutch Sports discussed a number of teams his client was considering on his “Game Over” podcast with Max Kellerman.

On a white board, Paul and Kellerman went through the pros of James signing with each team and their starting rosters. The list included:

According to a report by Charania, those teams seemed to have been cut down to the Cavaliers, Heat and 76ers.

Charania said executives for interested teams have been sending voice notes to Paul, who passed them along to James.

“There's a focus on Cleveland, Miami and Philadelphia," Charania said in a July 14 report. " ... This could be any day, any week, now," but that James was getting closer to a decision.

@ShamsCharania latest update on LeBron James' free agency 👑

“There's a focus on Cleveland, Miami and Philadelphia." pic.twitter.com/0P3Cv0F93O

According to Kalshi, here's when James is expected to sign with a new team:

While appearing with 97.5 The Fanantic in Philadelphia last week, ESPN's Dave McMenamin said it was likely that James would make his decision this week.

"A source familiar with LeBron really believes LeBron is nearing the decision ... People around the league are hoping that it comes next week," McMenamin said.

"... I would say there is a pretty decent chance we find out next week. I do believe, strongly, that the Sixers are a legitimate option for him still. I was told, as of last night, he still hasn't made his final decision."

A report the same day by Charania said he spoke with Paul the morning of July 20 and that there's no decision by James and no timetable on a decision. Charania said his understanding is focus remains on Miami, Cleveland, Philadelphia.

However, Paul said on his “Game Over” podcast on July 20 that there is no timetable for James to sign with a new team.

“We’re not going to be rushed,” Paul said.

Speculation that the Heat are now the frontrunners for James' services after Miami jumped ahead of Cleveland in the prediction markets over the weekend.

During a July 16 report, Charania said James' decision was expected soon.

"I think this process is getting closer and closer to the end here," Charania said. "LeBron James has all the information that he needs."

BREAKING: LeBron James is rumoured to be signing with a team VERY soon, per @ShamsCharania.

📈 Shams says that LeBron, after facing criticism for taking too long, has all the information to make a final choice.

There are rumours that LeBron could be doing a "big event" for it. pic.twitter.com/6KK2T8udZN

— BallersCulture (@BallersCultureX) July 17, 2026

Reports were speculating that James would announce his signing during Fanatics Fest, which was held last week in the Javits Center in New York.

But during events on July 16 and 17, James declined to say if had made a free-agent decision yet.

Tyrese Haliburton to LeBron James: 

“What are you going on, what is this… Year 76?”

I see what you did there 😏 pic.twitter.com/RMv5kzYeMw

— RB (@RBPhillyTake) July 16, 2026

"I won't hold you guys up too much longer," James said.

During his Fanatics appearance, James even said the phrase "trust the process," which Sixers fans were quick to connect to the Sixers' longtime rebuilding era with Embiid.

As LeBron James rattles off what he will value in his next team, he used the phrase “trust the process” and the crowd was sent into a tizzy, thinking he was giving a clue about the Philadelphia 76ers. pic.twitter.com/klpBWePWNq

— Dave McMenamin (@mcten) July 17, 2026

Another report over the weekend stated that Sixers owner Josh Harris also spoke to James briefly during Fanatics Fest.

Bob Cooney of On Pattison said on July 20 that he has a strong feeling that James will sign with Philadelphia.

"I am getting a strong feeling he is coming here. I really am," Cooney said. "Why is a 42-year-old man going out there and throwing out 'Trust the Process' and '76 Years,' and all these things that are little hints?

"… Why would you go around teasing stuff like that? … Why are insiders hinting at it now? … Over the last couple of days, I started thinking, you know what, he is putting it out there. I think he might be coming to the Sixers. A couple of weeks ago, you asked me for a percentage, and I said 15-20%. I am upping that now; I think it is 50-50 at least."

During his appearance on 97.5 The Fanantic in Philadelphia, McMenamin said it's top Sixers players Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey and newly acquired forward Jaylen Brown who are currently in contact with James. and not Philly brass such as Bob Meyers, Mike Gansey and Nick Nurse.

"I want to say the people that I've spoken to told me about (Joel) Embiid's interaction, it's been mostly through text messaging, which is an obvious way to communicate in 2026," McMenamin said. "As far as my understanding, it's really been (Tyrese) Maxey as the primary spearhead in terms of keeping that communication and dialogue going. Maxey knows LeBron the best out of those three players

"… I've covered a bunch of teams, and it's not very often where you get all three, if you do have three stars, on the same page moving in the same direction," he said. "It's significant that, from the top down, Bob Myers, Nick Nurse, Mike Gansey, and the three best players on the team are all together on this, saying, 'LeBron, we want you, we need you, and we can do great things if you come here.'"

If James were to sign with Philadelphia, the Sixers would potentially have one of the most talented starting lineups in the NBA. Here's what it might look it:

Sixers potential starting lineup

title: Hall of Fame Ex-Teammate Predicts LeBron James’ Next Team
description: A Hall of Fame former teammate has weighed in on where LeBron James should play next, offering a prediction about the NBA superstar's future. The post Hall of Fame Ex-Teammate Predicts LeBron James’ Next Team appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
LeBron James’ future remains one of the NBA’s biggest offseason storylines, and now one of his Hall of Famer former teammates has shared where he believes the four-time MVP should play next.

The recommendation reflects a broader vision for how James could shape the final chapter of his legendary career, but not everyone is likely to agree with it.

That teammate is Chris Bosh, James teammate for four years with the Miami Heat. Bosh’s advice to James? Return to Miami. Bosh has not spoken to James directly about the decision. That hasn’t stopped him from making the pitch publicly, delivered with the same dry humor that defined his playing days.

“When you’re older, when you’re ready to retire, you go to Miami,” Bosh joked in an interview with Front Office Sports. Bosh himself retired due to a health scare, making the 2015-16 season the last in which he played. The 6-foot-11 center was inducted into the Hall of Fame in 2021.

Bosh sees a Heat roster that could pair James with Bam Adebayo and the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to Miami, comparing that frontcourt combination’s paint pressure to the dynamic he and Dwyane Wade once created running alongside James, leading to two NBA championships, in 2012 and 2013.

Read More From Heavy New NBA Exec Survey: LeBron James Will Snub Hometown Cavaliers For One Team

James, 41, told the Los Angeles Lakers in late June he would not return for 2026-27, informing the organization it could move forward without him this offseason, setting up a record 24th year in the league elsewhere.

The Cleveland Cavaliers, Golden State Warriors, and Philadelphia 76ers have all been floated as landing spots along with Miami. James has made no decision and set no deadline. Klutch Sports founder Rich Paul, James’ longtime agent, said on his own podcast this week that the process would not be rushed, framing it as a business decision rather than a spectacle, according to SI.com‘s reporting.

Bosh isn’t buying into the idea that legacy weighs on the decision at all.

“I think legacy and reputation, that’s already built,” Bosh said, according to Front Office Sports. “He’s earned the right to make his own decision.”

Bosh knows the free-agency dance because he lived it. Toronto drafted him fourth overall out of Georgia Tech in 2003, and he spent seven seasons building the Raptors into a playoff team before leaving for Miami in the 2010 free agency class that also landed James and Wade. The trio won back-to-back titles in 2012 and 2013.

Bosh made 11 All-Star teams and won Olympic gold with Team USA in 2008. His career ended prematurely in 2016 after recurring blood clots forced him off the floor for good. He officially retired in 2019.

Across 893 games, Bosh averaged 19.2 points and 8.5 rebounds while shooting 49.4 percent from the floor. He’s since written a book on athletic development and stays connected to the game through charity work, including a USA Basketball three-on-three event next month.

Whether James takes his old running mate’s advice remains to be seen. Miami, at least, has an alumnus lobbying for him.

title: Miami mistakenly post James 'introductory' video
description: Miami Heat mistakenly post a link on YouTube to LeBron James' 'introductory press conference' despite the NBA great not yet deciding where he will play next season.
fullText:
Miami Heat mistakenly posted a link on YouTube to LeBron James' 'introductory press conference' despite the NBA great not yet deciding where he will play next season.

The link to the live stream - scheduled for 27 July - was uploaded to the Heat's channel on Tuesday before being deleted.

A Heat spokesperson told Reuters, external the video was posted in error by staff who were preparing for the possibility of James joining the side.

BBC Sport has contacted Miami Heat for comment.

James, 41, announced he was leaving the Los Angeles Lakers in June after eight years, in which he led them to the 2020 NBA title - his fourth overall.

He previously played for Miami between 2010 and 2014.

James averaged 20.9 points, 7.2 assists and 6.1 rebounds last season with the Lakers.

The 22-time All-Star is the NBA's all-time leader in games (1,622) and points (43,440).

A return to Florida for James would form a formidable starting five for the Heat alongside fellow new signing and two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo and centre Bam Adebayo.

Given the Heat traded key members of their squad away to land Antetokounmpo from the Milwaukee Bucks, a player of James' calibre would add further scoring power and experience with the Miami team focused on a quick rebuild towards a title push.

But earlier this week, James' agent Rich Paul said he is "not going to be rushed" into making a decision.

"It's his choice to make and when he makes the choice, he'll make it," Paul said on the Game Over with Max Kellerman and Rich Paul podcast.

title: Dayton basketball: Holmes 'ready to attack' second season on court in NBA
description: Jul. 22—KETTERING — As DaRon Holmes II signed autographs and posed for photos with Dayton Flyers fans at Lee's Famous Recipe Chicken, someone approached him and said, "Welcome back to Dayton. This is home, right?" "Just like you just said," Holmes said on Tuesday, July 21. "This is my other home.
fullText:
Jul. 22—KETTERING — As DaRon Holmes II signed autographs and posed for photos with Dayton Flyers fans at Lee's Famous Recipe Chicken, someone approached him and said, "Welcome back to Dayton. This is home, right?"

"Just like you just said," Holmes said on Tuesday, July 21. "This is my other home. It's always great to be back. I'm so comfortable here. The people here are always amazing with the hospitality. I'm just grateful I remember when it all first started."

Holmes arrived at the University of Dayton as a freshman five years ago. Now he's entering his third year in the NBA with the Denver Nuggets. This was his second appearance in as many years at Lee's, which he endorsed throughout his college career.

Holmes will spend a couple of days in Dayton, his visit overlapping with Obi Toppin's, Dayton's other recent All-American forward. Toppin will host a golf event, the Obi Open, on Wednesday, July 22, and then a sixth annual youth basketball camp on Thursday at the UD RecPlex.

Holmes hopes to return to Dayton during the 2026-27 season for a game at UD Arena. He hasn't had that opportunity since his final game with the Flyers in 2024.

"It's just hard because of our season," he said. "It's just very difficult. But I'm going to try my best."

The last time Holmes returned to Dayton, he had yet to play in an NBA game. He missed what would have been his rookie season after tearing his Achilles tendon in the NBA Summer League in 2024.

Holmes finally made his NBA debut last season. He appeared in 25 games, averaging 3.7 points and 1.4 rebounds in 8.4 minutes per game. He also appeared in 13 NBA G League games with the Grand Rapids Gold, averaging 20.9 points and 7.8 rebounds.

"It was fun," Holmes said. "I learned a lot after tearing my Achilles, just watching the games. It was weird battling two things at once. Am I going to be re-injured? And it's my first year in the NBA. It's like those two things were aligned, and it was very difficult to balance. But, overall, I gained great experience. I'm ready to attack this next year. I'm very excited."

Holmes did not play in the NBA Summer League this month but did practice with the Nuggets players on the Summer League roster in Las Vegas.

"It was a very valuable experience," he said. "I wish I could have played. It wasn't my decision, ultimately."

Holmes did play pickup basketball with NBA veterans in Las Vegas.

"I had a good showing," he said, "so that was fun. I'm just taking things one step at a time, doing anything I can."

Holmes reunited with Dayton head coach Anthony Grant and associate head coach James Kane in Vegas. They had dinner with former Flyers Toumani Camara and Nate Santos and former UD graduate assistant Leron Black, who's now an assistant coach with the Portland Trail Blazers, Camara's team.

Holmes watched the Flyers on TV often last season. He played a pickup game with them on campus during his visit to UD in 2025. He said he's ready for Dayton's latest big man with star potential, 7-foot-1 center Amaël L'Etang, to have a breakout season as a junior.

Holmes also knows one of the new Flyers well. He played at Montverde Academy in the 2020-21 season at the same time as 6-7 forward Jalen Haynes, a Cincinnati transfer who signed with Dayton in the spring. Holmes was a senior that season. Haynes spent a post-graduate year at Montverde after graduating from Dillard High School in Fort Lauderdale, Fla., in 2020.

"We used to scrimmage and practice a lot," Holmes said. "We have a good history. I'm glad he's going there. I think he's a very vital piece. He's going to be huge. He's physical. He's a good leader, too."

title: Chris Bosh on Health Big Contracts and LeBron’s Future
description: Chris Bosh on LeBron to Miami, NBA expansion, blood clot awareness, big contract realities, and NIL.
fullText:
Chris Bosh joins Dan Roberts of Front Office Sports to discuss:

Why he thinks LeBron James should go to Miami and team up with Giannis

title: FIFA World Cup breaks 32-year Fox and Telemundo record
description: Soccer is perhaps the most popular sport in the world, even if it is not the biggest in the United States of America. The NFL, NBA, etc, are generally more popular than ‘football’, but the 2026 World Cup meant a lot more eyes were on soccer across America this summer.
fullText:
Soccer is perhaps the most popular sport in the world, even if it is not the biggest in the United States of America.

The NFL, NBA, etc, are generally more popular than ‘football’, but the 2026 World Cup meant a lot more eyes were on soccer across America this summer.

In turn, it is perhaps not surprising to see the tournament breaking viewership records on Fox and Telemundo.

The USMNT were knocked out of the 2026 World Cup in the Round of 16 after losing 4-1 to Belgium.

Despite the early exit, it seems like many were still heavily invested in the competition all the way up to the final.

This was made clear after Fox’s Michael Mulvihill tweeted that the final between Spain and Argentina set viewership records on the network.

He stated: “The World Cup Final averaged 66.4 million viewers in the U.S. on FOX and Telemundo combined. 42.5 million on FOX. 23.9 million on Telemundo.

“Other than Super Bowls, this is the largest sports audience since the 1994 Winter Olympics.”

Soccer in America still has some way to go if it is to reach the heights of the Super Bowl, however.

The 2026 showpiece is reported to have had over 125 million viewers on Fox earlier this year, around double that of the World Cup.

Of course, that may have been different had the USMNT reached the final, but that was always going to be an unlikely scenario.

Still, it may lead to more people inside the USA starting to take soccer a bit more seriously, given there is clearly a decent audience to capitalise on.

title: When will Lebron make his decision? Lebron James next team odds
description: The Philadelphia 76ers are among a handful of teams making a pitch to sign free the NBA legend. See Lebron James next team odds.
fullText:
Do the Philadelphia 76ers really have a shot?

Following the announcement he wouldn't re-sign with the Los Angeles Lakers, NBA legend LeBron James has made it clear to teams that he would consider signing with a franchise that best fit his needs.

What those are aren't exactly clear, but the Sixers are reportedly one of the teams in the mix for James' services for the upcoming 2026-2027 NBA season — and possibly beyond.

See where the Sixers currently stand among James' suitors.

As of July 21, the Sixers ranked among the top possible teams to sign the all-time NBA leading scorer, according to prediction market Kalshi.

Here's where they rank as of 9 a.m. ET July 21:

Cleveland Cavaliers 27% (down 5%)

Minnesota Timberwolves 2% (down 1%)

Here's the breakdown of James' current odds of signing, according to market prediction site Polymarket:

Cleveland Cavaliers 27% (down 14%)

Philadelphia 76ers 14% (up 12%)

Golden State Warriors 11% (down 15%)

While most believe James will return to his hometown Cleveland to close out his career, there's a chance a he turns down a third reunion with the Cavs and signs elsewhere.

Recently, James' agent Rich Paul of Klutch Sports discussed a number of teams his client was considering on his “Game Over” podcast with Max Kellerman.

On a white board, Paul and Kellerman went through the pros of James signing with each team and their starting rosters. The list included:

According to a report by ESPN's Shams Charania, those teams seemed to have been cut down to the Cavaliers, Heat and 76ers.

Charania said executives for interested teams have been sending voice notes to Paul, who passed them along to James.

“There's a focus on Cleveland, Miami and Philadelphia," Charania said in a July 14 report. " ... This could be any day, any week, now," but that James was getting closer to a decision.

@ShamsCharania latest update on LeBron James' free agency 👑

“There's a focus on Cleveland, Miami and Philadelphia." pic.twitter.com/0P3Cv0F93O

According to Kalshi, here's when James is expected to sign with a new team:

While appearing with 97.5 The Fanantic in Philadelphia last week, ESPN's Dave McMenamin said it was likely that James would make his decision this week.

"A source familiar with LeBron really believes LeBron is nearing the decision ... People around the league are hoping that it comes next week," McMenamin said.

"... I would say there is a pretty decent chance we find out next week. I do believe, strongly, that the Sixers are a legitimate option for him still. I was told, as of last night, he still hasn't made his final decision."

A report the same day by Charania said he spoke with Paul the morning of July 20 and that there's no decision by James and no timetable on a decision. Charania said his understanding is focus remains on Miami, Cleveland, Philadelphia.

However, Paul said on his “Game Over” podcast on July 20 that there is no timetable for James to sign with a new team.

“We’re not going to be rushed,” Paul said.

Speculation that the Heat are now the frontrunners for James' services after Miami jumped ahead of Cleveland in the prediction markets over the weekend.

During a July 16 report, Charania said James' decision was expected soon.

"I think this process is getting closer and closer to the end here," Charania said. "LeBron James has all the information that he needs."

BREAKING: LeBron James is rumoured to be signing with a team VERY soon, per @ShamsCharania.

📈 Shams says that LeBron, after facing criticism for taking too long, has all the information to make a final choice.

There are rumours that LeBron could be doing a "big event" for it. pic.twitter.com/6KK2T8udZN

— BallersCulture (@BallersCultureX) July 17, 2026

Reports were speculating that James would announce his signing during Fanatics Fest, which was held last week in the Javits Center in New York.

But during events on July 16 and 17, James declined to say if had made a free-agent decision yet.

Tyrese Haliburton to LeBron James: 

“What are you going on, what is this… Year 76?”

I see what you did there 😏 pic.twitter.com/RMv5kzYeMw

— RB (@RBPhillyTake) July 16, 2026

"I won't hold you guys up too much longer," James said.

During his Fanatics appearance, James even said the phrase "trust the process," which Sixers fans were quick to connect to the Sixers' longtime rebuilding era with Embiid.

As LeBron James rattles off what he will value in his next team, he used the phrase “trust the process” and the crowd was sent into a tizzy, thinking he was giving a clue about the Philadelphia 76ers. pic.twitter.com/klpBWePWNq

— Dave McMenamin (@mcten) July 17, 2026

Another report over the weekend stated that Sixers owner Josh Harris also spoke to James briefly during Fanatics Fest.

Bob Cooney of On Pattison said on July 20 that he has a strong feeling that James will sign with Philadelphia.

"I am getting a strong feeling he is coming here. I really am," Cooney said. "Why is a 42-year-old man going out there and throwing out 'Trust the Process' and '76 Years,' and all these things that are little hints?

"… Why would you go around teasing stuff like that? … Why are insiders hinting at it now? … Over the last couple of days, I started thinking, you know what, he is putting it out there. I think he might be coming to the Sixers. A couple of weeks ago, you asked me for a percentage, and I said 15-20%. I am upping that now; I think it is 50-50 at least."

During his appearance on 97.5 The Fanantic in Philadelphia, McMenamin said it's top Sixers players Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey and newly acquired forward Jaylen Brown who are currently in contact with James. and not Philly brass such as Bob Meyers, Mike Gansey and Nick Nurse.

"I want to say the people that I've spoken to told me about (Joel) Embiid's interaction, it's been mostly through text messaging, which is an obvious way to communicate in 2026," McMenamin said. "As far as my understanding, it's really been (Tyrese) Maxey as the primary spearhead in terms of keeping that communication and dialogue going. Maxey knows LeBron the best out of those three players

"… I've covered a bunch of teams, and it's not very often where you get all three, if you do have three stars, on the same page moving in the same direction," he said. "It's significant that, from the top down, Bob Myers, Nick Nurse, Mike Gansey, and the three best players on the team are all together on this, saying, 'LeBron, we want you, we need you, and we can do great things if you come here.'"

If James were to sign with Philadelphia, the Sixers would potentially have one of the most talented starting lineups in the NBA. Here's what it might look it:

Sixers potential starting lineup

title: LeBron James replies to Tyrese Haliburton’s Pacers pitch
description: Several NBA stars are reportedly doing their best to lure LeBron James to join them, including Tyrese Haliburton. The Indiana Pacers guard revealed that he pitched to him via a text message, and the free agent forward replied to him.…
fullText:
Several NBA stars are reportedly doing their best to lure LeBron James to join them, including Tyrese Haliburton. The Indiana Pacers guard revealed that he pitched to him via a text message, and the free agent forward replied to him.

However, it’s not what Haliburton hoped for. James didn’t use words; instead, he sent six emojis to sum up how he feels about taking his talents to Indianapolis

MORE: LeBron James NBA sweepstakes has familiar team emerge as heavy favorite

“LeBron being my teammate isn’t looking good,” Haliburton said. “I texted him, ‘Hey, you should come be a Pacer.’ He responded with six laughing crying faces.”

The Miami Heat, Cleveland Cavaliers, Philadelphia 76ers, and Golden State Warriors reportedly lead the race to secure the 22-time All-Star’s signature. Reports also suggested that money does not influence James’ decision the most, but where he will have the best chance to win his fifth championship.

James was initially rumored to make his free agency decision early this month but the NBA world continues to await his announcement. His camp has remained mum on providing an exact time and date.

Many believe the Heat are the favorites to land him; however, joining Joel Embiid and Jaylen Brown in Philadelphia is gaining traction. Golden State also has a fighting chance as James shares a strong friendship with Draymond Green and Stephen Curry. It remains to be seen which team he will play for next season, but what’s certain is that his decision will change the league’s landscape.

title: Kings’ Acuff Expected To Score More Than 2021-22 Brunson
description: Analysts predict high-volume scoring for Sacramento’s seventh overall pick, but inconsistent efficiency remains a concern as the rookie studies Jalen Brunson’s film to master elite NBA footwork.
fullText:
Sactown Sports radio host Carmichael Dave has set Jalen Brunson's fourth year in the NBA as the standard for Darius Acuff to surpass in his rookie season with the Sacramento Kings.

At the time, Brunson averaged 16.3 points for the Dallas Mavericks in the 2021-22 season. Dave expects Acuff to average more than Brunson did, but would be "falling out of my chair" shocked if he accomplishes his goal efficiently.

"I fully expect Darius Acuff to average in the 17-18 point range," Dave told his co-host, Jason Russ, on "The Carmichael Dave Show" on Monday. "But I would also be shocked if Darius Acuff shot anything above 42%, probably high 30s."

Sacramento's seventh overall pick wasn't an effective scorer in Summer League. He failed to shoot 50% or better from the field in every game, except in the final appearance against the Brooklyn Nets.

Acuff put together some abysmal shooting performances: 9-for-29 in the California Classic opener, 7-for-19 against the Milwaukee Bucks, 6-for-20 in the Las Vegas Summer League opener and 4-for-14 against the Washington Wizards. That totals out to 31.8% overall.

The 6-foot-3 guard showed promise getting to his favorite spots on the floor, setting up his defenders with nifty dribble moves and blowing past them. He just couldn't convert, especially from three-point range.

As a small guard who isn't the most athletic player on the court, he has to rely on his craftiness to score. Size definitely played a role in Acuff's poor efficiency.

There is room for improvement for the former Arkansas Razorback, though. Receiving comparisons to Brunson influenced him to break down the New York Knicks' star's film from the NBA Finals.

“I’ve been focusing on both teams, of course, but really been seeing how Jalen Brunson is navigating through the defense and stuff like that," Acuff Jr. told Draymond Green on "The Draymond Green Show" on June 7. "Obviously, as a PG myself, I’ve been paying attention to that.”

Brunson's footwork is part of what makes him such an efficient scorer. When he picks up his dribble in a crowded paint, he twists and turns to see which way he wants to score. If a defender bites on the pump fake, he steps in for a floater; if he creates enough separation with a shoulder bump, he'll shoot a turnaround jumper.

Acuff can learn from his comparison's grounded scoring style. It allows him to absorb contact without being pushed off his spot, and continue surveying the defense for scoring or passing opportunities.

The former Razorback could meet Dave's expectations, especially since he led the SEC in scoring this past season at 23.5 points. His efficiency will take some refining, but he'll adjust to the NBA's pace sooner rather than later.

title: Former top-five pick in 2015 eyes NBA comeback amid free agency
description: A former top-five pick in 2015, Mario Hezonja is looking to make an NBA return. Hezonja last played in the NBA during the 2019-20 season.
fullText:
A former No. 5 overall pick by the Orlando Magic in the 2015 NBA Draft, Mario Hezonja seems intent on making an NBA comeback. According to author and insider Chema de Lucas, Hezonja informed Real Madrid that he will be leaving the club for the NBA.

However, no NBA team has officially signed the Croatian forward. In five NBA seasons between the Magic, Knicks and Trail Blazers, Hezonja averaged 6.9 points, 3.1 rebounds, 1.3 assists and 0.7 steals in 330 games, never living up to the hype of a top-five selection. During the 2025-26 season with Real Madrid, Hezonja averaged 17.5 points, 4.9 rebounds and 1.9 assists in 28 games. Hezonja is still just 31 years old.

title: $3 Wilt Chamberlain Lakers jacket from 1972 NBA Finals found in thrift store sells for $89K at auction
description: A warmup jacket worn by Wilt Chamberlain during the 1972 NBA Finals that was bought by Quinn Brown was sold for $89,600 at auction on Monday.
fullText:
A warmup jacket worn by Wilt Chamberlain during the 1972 NBA Finals that was bought by a teenager for $3.07 at an Oregon thrift store sold for $89,600 at auction on Monday.

Quinn Brown had been eyeing a massive bin of clothes at a Goodwill store outside Portland in January when he saw someone toss a Lakers jacket with Chamberlain’s name on it back on the pile.

Brown, who resells used clothes online, quickly grabbed it.

The jacket was auctioned by Sotheby’s, which had estimated its value between $150,000 and $250,000 before bidding closed Monday.

It received a total of 48 bids, according to the auction house.

Described on Sotheby’s website as an “extraordinary piece of memorabilia,” the short-sleeved yellow warmup jacket was worn by Chamberlain during the 1972 NBA finals, in which the Los Angeles Lakers beat the New York Knicks.

“I didn’t even think that finding something like this was possible,” Brown said.

Chamberlain, an NBA Hall of Famer viewed as one of the all-time greats, played in the league from 1959 to 1973.

He won two championship rings — one with the Philadelphia 76ers in 1967, and another with the Los Angeles Lakers in 1972.

He holds the record for most points scored by a player in a single game, netting 100 points in a 1962 match.

For the past three years, Brown has been reselling clothes online after finding them in thrift stores.

The most expensive item he had sold previously was a T-shirt of Sub Pop Records, the indie label that signed Nirvana in 1989 and released the band’s first album. It went for $250.

After buying the Chamberlain jacket back in January, Brown searched online to see if it was worth something.

He found a picture on Google of Chamberlain wearing such a jacket and compared it to the one he had.

In examining the Lakers patch on the front, he noticed certain small stitching details that matched the photo.

He also looked up the measurements of a different jersey worn by Chamberlain, a towering figure at 7 feet, 1 inch (2.2 meters) tall, and discovered that they nearly aligned.

“I really just wanted to make sure, because it was so hard to believe at the time,” he said, adding that he had only ever read about such serendipitous thrift findings.

When he posted a photo of the jacket on social media, Sotheby’s contacted him about consigning the jacket to the auction house. Brown agreed, and an armored van came to pick it up.

Sotheby’s then did its own photo verification and determined that the jacket was worn by Chamberlain during the 1972 Finals, in which he was named the Finals MVP.

“Photomatching a piece of game-worn clothing to a specific Finals series, for a player of this era, is genuinely exceptional,” Sotheby’s website says.

“As photomatched items worn by Chamberlain become exceedingly rare, this jacket is a piece of sports history.”

It is not the first Chamberlain item from the 1972 finals to fetch a hefty sum under the hammer: A game jersey he wore during the series sold for $4.9 million at Sotheby’s in 2023, the fourth most expensive NBA jersey ever sold by the auction house.

As for his thrifted find, Brown said: “That was as good as it gets.”

title: Former 76ers defensive specialist signs with Lakers amid NBA free agency
description: After seven NBA seasons between the Philadelphia 76ers and Portland Trail Blazers, Matisse Thybulle has agreed to a one-year deal with the Lakers.
fullText:
Former Philadelphia 76ers forward Matisse Thybulle has officially joined his third NBA franchise, signing a one-year $3.3 million pact with the Los Angeles Lakers, according to Shams Charania. Ever since a draft night trade sent him from the Celtics to the 76ers, Thybulle has been one of the league's better defensive specialists, making the All-Defensive Second Team twice in Philadelphia. He was later traded to the West Coast in a deal that included both Josh Hart and Jalen McDaniels.

Recently with the Portland Trail Blazers, Thybulle has had trouble staying healthy, suiting up in only 45 games over the past two seasons. That said, Thybulle's 3-point shooting has improved since leaving the Sixers, as the former No. 20 overall selection made 37.4% of his attempts with Portland. Now playing a support role to Luka Doncic and the Lakers, Thybulle will see plenty of open looks on the perimeter.

This article originally appeared on Sixers Wire: Former 76ers defensive specialist signs with Lakers amid NBA free agency

title: Former Spartans guard makes NBA Summer League debut this weekend
description: See how former Spartans guard Tre Holloman fared in this NBA Summer League debut on Saturday
fullText:
A former Spartans point guard made his NBA Summer League debut this past weekend in a limited role off the bench.

Former Michigan State point guard Tre Holloman played in his first NBA Summer League game on Saturday, scoring two points and two assists in five minutes of action with the Orlando Magic. He ended the game with a plus/minus of four points as well.

Holloman played his first three years of his collegiate career with the Spartans before transferring to NC State for this past season. He appeared in 106 games during his three years with the Spartans, averaging 9.1 points and 3.7 assists per game during his final season in 2024-25.

In his lone year with NC State, Holloman appeared in 32 games (27 of which were starts). He ended the season averaging 9.2 points and 2.1 assists per game with the Wolfpack.

title: NBA Reddit thinks Cooper Flagg grew 2 inches
description: This would be big news for the star out of Duke.
fullText:
NBA Reddit thinks Cooper Flagg grew 2 inches originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Cooper Flagg is already a rising NBA superstar.

If the Dallas Mavericks' phenom out of Duke grew a couple inches? He'd be even better.

That's the theory that's been going around the Internet for a while this summer.

Some folks on X were posting about it. There's a discussion thread on Reddit about it, too.

Internet sleuths noticed that Flagg liked a post on Instagram that said he grew two inches, from 6-foot-9 to 6-foot-11.

There have been no public comments on the matter, though, and no official listings anywhere that show Flagg at that taller height.

So at this point, it's all a bit of speculation.

MORE: Caleb Wilson fires a subtle shot at AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson

Presumably at some point, either Flagg will be asked about it or the Mavs will release an official roster with a height listing.

In the meantime, commenters on that Reddit post are speculating that Flagg looks taller next to his teammate Dereck Lively II than he did last year.

It wouldn't be crazy if Flagg was still growing. He's just 19 and won't turn 20 years old until late December.

Most players don't change their height listing much, if at all, after entering the NBA. But the Spurs' Victor Wembanyama did, and it's certainly not unheard of.

Some players actually grow more and don't change their height. Kevin Garnett was almost certainly taller than he was listed at, for example.

The reality is that if Flagg stars on the court, it doesn't really matter what his basketball card says he is.

But if he's actually taller, opponents should look out. That makes Flagg that much scarier to deal with.

title: The NBA’s Next Major Labor Battle May Already Be Brewing
description: The NBPA and NBA commissioner Adam Silver are already staking out opposite positions about the second apron ahead of the next round of CBA negotiations.
fullText:
Victor Wembanyama might have been the straw that broke the camel's back in the NBA.

Wembanyama bypassed the chance at starting his new contract extension at 30% of the 2027-28 salary cap, which could cost him more than $50 million in total. He "chose a contract sacrifice rooted in giving him and the organization increased ability to build a sustained title contender around him," according to ESPN's Shams Charania.

A week before Wembanyama agreed to that contract, the Boston Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the division-rival Philadelphia 76ers for a surprisingly meager package, which infuriated much of their fanbase. Team president Brad Stevens later explained that he thought the Celtics path to getting back to championship contention "looked a little bit more challenging with 70% of our cap and such a high percent of our usage tied into two players [Brown and Jayson Tatum."

That appears to have caught the attention of both the National Basketball Players Association and players around the NBA. And with the negotiations over the league's next collective bargaining agreement fast approaching, that could prove to be the foundation of an explosive battle between the NBA and the players' union.

On July 3, Milwaukee Bucks forward Kyle Kuzma posted a long tweet railing against the impact of the current CBA.

A week later, NBPA executive director David Kelly promised that the players union would "do a better job of fighting back" against the second apron moving forward.

"We are not fans of the second apron," Kelly said, per ESPN's Ben Golliver. "We did not propose the second apron. We should have done a better job of fighting back against the second apron. In the future, we will have a much more unified union, and we will do a better job of fighting back. ... We're seeing [the apron system] decimate teams and force decisions to be made that are not basketball decisions."

The players are poised to run into a problem, though. The league seems perfectly content with how things are playing out.

"The purpose of the system is ultimately to create competition throughout the league, and from that standpoint, I think the system is working incredibly well," NBA commissioner Adam Silver said after the board of governors meeting Tuesday.

A reporter asked Silver whether seeing teams have to trade stars for financial reasons made him want to change something about the CBA because of that "unintended consequence." Silver replied, "It's certainly not an unintended consequence."

"When you have a salary system in place as we do, every general manager is going to need to make mixed basketball and business decisions. Frankly, they make them regardless of whether you have a cap. You see that in other sports. People manage to budgets. People recognize that you can’t — at some point, you can’t have unlimited resources, whether it’s for a team or any business.

"In the case of a league, it’s in essence zero sum. So to the extent that [a team] doesn’t re-sign a player or chooses to trade a player, of course that player goes to another team."

In other words: The NBA doesn’t care whether teams feel like they have to trade stars because they can’t afford two or three players on max contracts at once anymore. The same number of stars will be in the league, and better dispersing them throughout all 30 markets could help revive interest across the country.

Shortly before the new CBA took effect in 2023, Silver specifically addressed how he envisioned parity helping the NBA at large.

"When you think about a 30-team league, and it’s not just the fans in those markets but fans increasingly all over the world who follow a team in that particular market maybe because they have an affinity for that style of play or a particular player on that team or some created connection to that city, you want a league where everyone feels that if the team that they are rooting for is well-managed and gets a little bit lucky, too, that’s necessary, that they can truly compete for championships," he told reporters.

"I think this increased parity we're seeing around the league is fantastic. It's part by design, too. Through successive collective bargaining agreements and the one we just negotiated, there's some new provisions in that one, as well, that we hope will help even the playing field to a certain extent."

Based on players’ reactions around the league, it’s fair to ask whether the NBA went overboard in its pursuit of that desired parity.

Stevens told reporters that the Celtics might not have traded Brown "if there was a rule in the CBA that said the guys that you drafted that you signed to 35% supermaxes count as 25% of the cap. Because then that would allow you to build out towards the aprons with a lot more flexibility."

Building through the draft isn’t the only pathway that got more complicated after the second apron took effect, though. Trades became far more difficult for teams over the first and second apron (particularly the second), and the CBA’s extension rules largely killed what was left of free agency. All three main routes became far more challenging.

From the NBA’s perspective, that’s a feature, not a bug. The CBA was designed to encourage more parity. Silver has been saying it for years. Although the NBA's most memorable eras tend to revolve around dynasties, the league office appears to want a more even distribution of talent throughout the league.

The roster-building restrictions for teams that cross the second apron effectively force their hands at a certain point. Even the Oklahoma City Thunder, who are still sitting on a huge haul of future draft capital, had to start trimming their supporting cast this offseason because Chet Holmgren and Jalen Williams’ max extensions kicked in.

That work is just beginning for them. In 2027-28, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander begins his supermax extension, which will cause his salary to jump by roughly $20 million. Cason Wallace will also be a restricted free agent if he doesn't agree to an extension by October. The additions of rookie first-round picks Aday Mara and Bennett Stirtz may help the Thunder patch some holes, but they have no choice but to continue sloughing off pieces from their roster for financial reasons.

The Thunder are hardly the only team that have run into this problem. The Celtics won a championship two years ago and have since blown apart the core of that team. The Orlando Magic likely aren't far behind. That's all by design.

The question is whether that's what's best for the league.

"Historically, one of the issues in our league was we didn’t necessarily want to break up teams," Silver told reporters in 2018. "There is a different sense in the NBA than the NFL, and the chemistry and dynamic that comes together with a group of players.”

He added at the time that the league "could do more to achieve parity, but you also don't want parity of mediocrity."

Is the NBA trending in that direction due to the second apron? That's a question for the players' union to wrestle with as it prepares to battle once again with team owners and the league office in the next round of CBA negotiations.

Unless otherwise noted, all stats via NBA.com, PBPStats, Cleaning the Glass or Basketball Reference. All salary information via Spotrac and salary-cap information via RealGM. All odds via FanDuel Sportsbook.

title: Clark 'a political football' says NBA chief Silver
description: NBA commissioner Adam Silver believes the debate around the treatment of Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark is "not largely about officiating".
fullText:
Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark has become a "political football" in the United States and the debate around fouls against the player are "not largely about officiating", NBA commissioner Adam Silver says.

Clark, 24, has become one of the biggest draws in the WNBA since being drafted by the Fever in 2024.

As a result, there has been a lot of attention paid to the way Clark is treated by opposing teams and the tactics they employ to try to limit her impact during games.

Last month, Phoenix Mercury forward Alyssa Thomas received a retrospective one-game ban and a $1,000 (£743) fine for appearing to knee Clark in the groin and push her fist into her neck during a tussle for possession.

It led to Fever coach Stephanie White labelling the officiating of games involving Clark in the WNBA as "egregious" and "utterly disrespectful", while Thomas said the incident with the 2024 Rookie of the Year led to her receiving death threats and racist abuse.

A group of 11 Republican lawmakers then sent a letter to WNBA commissioner Cathy Engelbert asking the league to take "accountability" and expressing concern that "attacks against Clark may be racially motivated".

Political commentators on the right have also weighed in, suggesting the treatment of Clark is rooted in racism and jealousy.

Speaking as part of a panel at an event in New York, Silver said the debates surrounding Clark had become about broader political and cultural issues in the United States rather than basketball alone.

"That particular incident is not about whether a foul should have been called at the time of the game or whether that was ultimately a flagrant non-review," said Silver.

"I've come to know Caitlin really well. She's an incredible player and also an incredible person.

"And she wants to focus on being the best player she can. And she's become a bit of a political football in this country, and I think it's incredibly unfair to her."

Silver refused to comment when asked whether reports that he had pressured Engelbert to suspend Thomas last month were true.

But while the 64-year-old said there was "no doubt" officiating in the WNBA needs to improve, he doubled down on his belief that the intense focus on incidents involving Clark had become a game of "political ping-pong".

"She's a young woman who's trying to improve her game and focus on being the best player she can be," added Silver.

"I don't even think it's fair to her that this has become a separate storyline about whether a foul should have been called at the time or whether it should have been ruled a flagrant foul after the fact."

Engelbert was also a panellist at the New York event and said: "The vitriol and everything that our players receive is unacceptable."

Coach White has previously criticised the tone of the debate and highlighted an increase in "toxicity, racism, homophobia" in the league, especially in online comments.

And speaking earlier this month Clark was critical of "the harassment, the hate" surrounding debate, adding: "None of that is OK. That goes for the opposing teams we play, that goes for my team-mates, that goes for my coaches."

Her own frustration boiled over this week when she yelled at a referee and used an expletive after decisions went against her team.

title: Pacers playing Pelicans in final NBA Summer League game on Saturday
description: The NBA assigned the Pacers a fifth NBA Summer League game against the Pelicans on Saturday night.
fullText:
The Pacers will play their fifth and final NBA Summer League game of 2026 against the Pelicans on Saturday night at 9 p.m. ET at the Pavilion in Las Vegas, the NBA announced early Friday morning.

The NBA determines each team's fifth game based on Summer League standings after their first four. Four teams qualify for the league semifinals -- in this case the Rockets, Grizzlies, Lakers and Warriors. Each team that fails to qualify is assigned a time slot and an opponent for a consolation game. The Pacers are 1-3 through four games and the Pelicans are 2-2.

Jalen Slawson is the Pacers' leading scorer through four Summer League games with 20.5 points per game. Guard Kobe Bufkin is leading the Pelicans with 21.3 points per game.

title: LeBron James jokes about playing for another 24 years despite having the longest NBA career ever
description: LeBron James has been winning the battle against Father Time in the NBA, and he believes he could continue the fight for another two decades. LeBron will be entering his 24th NBA season in 2026-27, adding another year to his already-established record of most NBA seasons played.
fullText:
LeBron James has been winning the battle against Father Time in the NBA, and he believes he could continue the fight for another two decades.

LeBron will be entering his 24th NBA season in 2026-27, adding another year to his already-established record of most NBA seasons played.

But if it was left up to the 41-year-old, he could be competitive on an NBA court for another 24 years.

During his live podcast with Tyrese Haliburton at the Fanatics Fest, Haliburton asked James how long could he realistically be a 10-point scorer in the NBA after averaging 20+ points at 41 years old.

“I can average 10 points and be in the league for… a long, long time… 65.”

James averaged 20.9 points, 7.2 assists and 6.1 rebounds last season, which was Year 23 for the No. 1 pick in the 2003 NBA Draft.

The all-time leading scorer will likely not continue playing in the NBA after another year or two to focus on his post-basketball life, but he probably could extend his career for many more years if he chose to.

He’s still putting up All-Star numbers and leading teams to Playoff series wins as the No. 1 option.

With him now being relegated to the third-best player on a team, LeBron could feasibly take another decade to regress out of a rotation on merit alone.

Even then, no player will be able to offer the kind of IQ James brings on the floor, further allowing him to play even longer.

This won’t happen. Even the great LeBron likely won’t have the mobility to spend five minutes on an NBA court at age 65, let alone score 10 points.

But this is an unprecedented battle against Father Time that we’re witnessing from LeBron. Unfortunately, the end comes for everyone, and James’ end might be upon us, despite his tall claims.

title: Miami Heat Receive Telling Hint as LeBron Reveals Vision for Next Team
description: It’s LeBron watch Day … something. (We lost count.) The NBA’s all time leading scorer has yet to choose a team, but he has begun dropping some hints. On Thursday, James made his first public appearance since informing the Los Angeles Lakers of his plans for the 2026-27 season. James, 41, ended his run with the […] The post Miami Heat Receive Telling Hint as LeBron Reveals Vision for Next Team appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
It’s LeBron watch Day … something. (We lost count.) The NBA’s all time leading scorer has yet to choose a team, but he has begun dropping some  hints.

On Thursday, James made his first public appearance since informing the Los Angeles Lakers of his plans for the 2026-27 season. James, 41, ended his run with the franchise after eight seasons.

The page has now turned, and James is hearing the noise loud and clear. He mentioned the Philadelphia 76ers, Golden State Warriors and Miami Heat as he played off the energy of the New York crowd. James didn’t reveal his decision, but he did let the world know (thanks to a young fan’s question) what he aims to deliver to his next team.

Heat general manager Pat Riley made it clear that the team is attempting to sign LeBron James in free agency.

Much has been speculated about what James is looking for in his next team, from wanting basketball happiness to competing for a fifth NBA championship. When asked about his free agency decision-making process, James revealed what he hopes to bring to the franchise he chooses to represent next.

“It’s a big decision,” James said Thursday. “… It’s a big decision for not only myself but my family as well and for the last part of my career and where I want to spend the last few years, or the last year, or the last two years of my NBA career. Where I go, I will make my staple on — I’m a natural born leader. Im’a try to fit into whatever team I’m going into but also give them all the tools and all the knowledge that I’ve been able to grasp over the last 23 years. I know the game. I know the ins and outs about the game of basketball.”

Plenty will get made about this response, but the least we know is that James wants to bring his psychological basketball expertise to the table. Could that mean James is eyeing a team that may be a leader away from winning a championship?

Giannis Antetokounmpo made his free agency pitch to LeBron James as rumors continue to link him to the Heat.

One plane has landed, Heat president Pat Riley says. The hope is to land another. Hopefully that one will carry James.

The Heat entered the offseason with strong intentions of making a needle-moving transaction. That goal was accomplished after the franchise acquired Giannis Antetokounmpo from the Milwaukee Bucks in a seismic trade last month.

Antetokounmpo, the 31-year-old two-time league MVP, vastly boosts the Heat’s chances of contending the Eastern Conference, but pairing him with James might just make the Heat a threat for the Larry O’Brien trophy.

James has had strong interest in playing alongside Antetokounmpo, according to reports, and this offseason gives the four-time MVP the best chance to achieve that wish. Antetokounmpo, too, has vision to play with the man he grew up watching as a kid in Greece.

It’s been over two weeks. James said he doesn’t plan on keeping us waiting much longer. We’ll see how well that statement holds up.

title: Jaden Akins makes late Summer League case for Knicks two-way roster spot
description: Add Jaden Akins to the list of names to watch of players the Knicks can consider for a two-way roster spot from their Las Vegas Summer League team. Akins, the 6-foot-4 guard who went undrafted in the 2025 NBA Draft before spending a season with the Detroit Pistons’ G-League Motor City Cruise team, had his breakout Summer League performance in the Knicks’ 87-77 loss to the Golden State Warriors ...
fullText:
Add Jaden Akins to the list of names to watch of players the Knicks can consider for a two-way roster spot from their Las Vegas Summer League team.

Akins, the 6-foot-4 guard who went undrafted in the 2025 NBA Draft before spending a season with the Detroit Pistons’ G-League Motor City Cruise team, had his breakout Summer League performance in the Knicks’ 87-77 loss to the Golden State Warriors on Thursday.

The 23-year-old scoring guard scored 21 points in 25 minutes off the bench and shot 9-of-20 from the field and three-of-eight from deep for his best performance of the Knicks’ first four games of Summer League play. Akins missed both Monday’s matchup against the Detroit Pistons and Saturday’s loss to the San Antonio Spurs. He scored just two points in 25 minutes in the Las Vegas opener against the Brooklyn Nets.

Akins, however, made a statement on Thursday, as the Knicks got little else from the rest of their Vegas squad — just nine points on 3-of-12 shooting from the field and one-of-seven shooting from 3-point range from Pacome Dadiet. 2026 Second-round pick Tyler Nickel shot three-of-six from deep for nine points and the bruising big man Liam Robbins added 13 points and nine rebounds.

But Thursday belonged to Akins — at least what was left of it after the Warriors had their way.

Golden State’s got a gem in Yaxel Lendeborg, the highly-touted lottery pick they selected 11th overall in the June NBA Draft who finished with three points, six rebounds and six assists in a standout defensive performance. The Warriors needed to win by at least two points to secure their Summer League playoff seed positioning.

They got little resistance from the Knicks, who didn’t have the firepower in a game second-year forward Mohamed Diawara missed. Second-round pick Jack Kayil got the start but finished with just four points on two-of-nine shooting from the field. Kayil finished with five rebounds, four assists and a steal but missed all seven of his attempts from behind the arc.

The Knicks have one more minimum contract slot available beneath the second apron to sign free agents in an effort to bolster the reserves around the championship core of Jalen Brunson, Karl-Anthony Towns, OG Anunoby, Mikal Bridges and Josh Hart. They also have until Oct. 31 to pick up the team option on the third year of Dadiet’s four-year rookie contract. Dadiet, the first-round pick the Knicks selected 25th overall in the 2024 NBA Draft, is scheduled to make roughly $3 million this season — enough space for another minimum salary player — with a pay raise north of $5 million in Year 4.

The Knicks must also make a decision on Dillon Jones, who won back-to-back championships in Oklahoma City and New York in his first two NBA seasons and was an invaluable member of head coach Mike Brown’s brain trust off the court. Jones started against the Warriors on Thursday and finished with five points on two-of-seven shooting from the field to go with seven rebounds and seven turnovers.

The Knicks turned the ball over 20 times in a sloppy game that featured 37 turnovers. They will play in a consolation Summer League game on Friday against the Detroit Pistons.

title: How the Lakers' standouts did in Thursday's summer league game
description: The Lakers once again put forth a fine performance during the NBA summer league on Thursday.
fullText:
The Los Angeles Lakers had the opportunity to win their fourth straight game of the main NBA summer league in Las Vegas when they took on the Chicago Bulls on Thursday. Once again, they didn't disappoint much.

After getting out to an early 8-2 lead, they outscored the Bulls 37-24 in the second quarter, and they never looked back. Los Angeles shot 49.2% from the field for the game and held Chicago to 38.5% shooting, resulting in a 105-82 win for the Purple and Gold.

Guard Chris Mañon continued to make a case that he may deserve some minutes with the Lakers in the NBA by leading his summer league squad in scoring with 16 points on 5-of-7 shooting. He also had six rebounds, one assist, one steal and one block, and he continued to play the type of tenacious defense he's known for.

Cameron Carr, the No. 24 pick in last month's draft, had 14 points on 5-of-12 overall shooting. He missed all three of his 3-point attempts, but he continues to look like he's ready for at least some small slivers of playing time once the NBA's regular season starts in October. He did a good job of moving without the ball, which got him some good looks, both from the mid-range area and near the basket.

Second-year forward Adou Thiero scored 11 points, and while he was 3-of-9 from the field, he continues to show gradual improvement. When driving to the basket, he's slowing down just enough to make better decisions, and he's passing the ball out more during his forays to the hoop when appropriate. Thiero also posted seven rebounds, four assists and two steals, and he continues to run the floor well in transition.

Off the bench, forward Arthur Kaluma continued his fine play with 14 points, four rebounds and three assists in 21 minutes. He's making a very strong case that he deserves a two-way contract.

For Chicago, forward Caleb Wilson, the No. 4 pick in this year's NBA draft, shot 7-of-15 overall and logged 21 points, eight rebounds, two assists, one steal and one block, although he committed eight turnovers.

The Lakers will head to the summer league playoffs, where they will play a semifinal round game on Saturday. The opponent and the time are to be determined.
